title: Hieracium albipes
taxonomy: Hieracium albipes is classified in the plant kingdom, specifically within the division Magnoliophyta and class Magnoliopsida. It belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. The genus Hieracium is a group of plants often referred to as hawkweeds due to their resemblance to the hawkweed genus. Within the genus Hieracium, the species Hieracium albipes is a distinct variety, identified by its unique characteristics and features. The classification of Hieracium albipes is further supported by its classification as a member of the order Asterales.

constituents:
I can find information on the active constituents of Hieracium albipes. 

Hieracium albipes contains sesquiterpene lactones such as 11,13-dihydroheterophyllin and 11,13-dihydroheterophyllin acetate, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. These compounds are also responsible for the plant's antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. Additionally, Hieracium albipes has been found to contain other bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. intro: Hieracium albipes, also known as white-petaled hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, offering potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium albipes include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Hieracium albipes is commonly prepared as a tincture or infusion, and is often used in herbal remedies to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
